name: resend-blade-mcp
title: Resend
description: Resend transactional email — send, contacts, broadcasts, templates, domains, webhooks. Security-first with no filesystem access.
tagline: Transactional email with zero filesystem exposure
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: RESEND_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Enable write operations (send, create, update, delete)

setup.help_text: Create an API key at resend.com → API Keys. Full access scope is recommended for all features.
setup.blurb: Security-first transactional email — no filesystem access, no API key CRUD exposed, credential scrubbing on all error paths.

highlights.text: Send — transactional and broadcast email with HTML or text
Contacts — manage audiences and contact lists
Broadcasts — send to audiences with scheduling
Templates — reusable email templates via API
Domains — verify and manage sending domains
Security-first — no filesystem access, credential scrubbing on errors

readme:
### What it does

Resend gives your AI agent the ability to send transactional email, manage contacts and audiences, create broadcasts, and administer sending domains — all through Resend’s developer-friendly API.

Your agent can send a welcome email, add contacts to an audience, schedule a broadcast, check domain verification status, and manage webhooks in a single conversation.

### Security model

This plugin was designed security-first:

- **No filesystem access** — the server never reads or writes files on disk
- **No API key CRUD** — cannot create, rotate, or delete API keys through tools
- **Credential scrubbing** — all error paths strip API keys and tokens before returning
- **Write gating** — send, create, update, and delete operations require `RESEND_WRITE_ENABLED=true`

Reads (list domains, get contacts, check email status) work immediately. Writes require the explicit opt-in.

### Token efficiency

API responses are trimmed to essential fields. Email send confirmations return just the ID and status. Contact lists return name and count without full member payloads.

### Tool coverage

| Category | Tools | What you can do |
|----------|-------|-----------------|
| Email | 2 | Send single email, batch send |
| Contacts | 4 | List, create, update, remove contacts |
| Audiences | 3 | List, create, delete audiences |
| Broadcasts | 3 | Create, send, list broadcasts |
| Domains | 3 | List, verify, check domain status |
| Webhooks | 3 | List, create, delete webhooks |

scenarios.label: Send a transactional notification email
scenarios.body: Compose and send an HTML email to a specific recipient using a verified sending domain. Check delivery status after sending to confirm receipt.

scenarios.label: Manage contacts and schedule a broadcast
scenarios.body: List existing audiences, add new contacts to a target audience, then create and schedule a broadcast email for the audience.

scenarios.label: Verify a new sending domain
scenarios.body: Add a new sending domain, retrieve the required DNS records for verification, and check verification status after DNS propagation.
